political.s: FEC: $307K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 10 executive donors.

labor.s: International Labor Rights Forum — 2019: Wage-theft accountability in Los Angeles garment supply chain.

charity.s: $38M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.07% of revenue) via TJX Foundation.

guns.s: Via parent company TJX Companies: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

privacy.s: 1 publicly-disclosed data breach per Have I Been Pwned (2007) — 94M accounts compromised. Most recent: TJX Companies (2007).

execPay.s: Via parent company TJX Companies: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 774:1 (median employee pay $14,994) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.industry: retail sale of textiles in specialised stores, consumer products distribution

health.s: 17 CPSC recalls (1 in past 24 months); latest hazard: fire & fire-related burn.

topHazards.label: The chargers can explode while in use, posing a fire and burn hazard.
The plastic pieces on the end of the pants' faux drawstrings can come loose and fall off, posing a choking hazard.
The wooden frame of the chair can break or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
The chairs can tip over or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Chargers Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Hazards; Sold by TJX at T.J. Maxx and Marshalls Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves magnetic wireless chargers sold under the Isla Rae brand. The chargers are compatible with magnetic charging systems and attach magnetically to the back of a phone to charge the device. The chargers were sold in the following colors: white, pink, and purple. The model number "RM5PBM" can be found …
hazards: The chargers can explode while in use, posing a fire and burn hazard.
units: About 13,200 (In addition, about 7,000 units were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Children's Brown Stretch Twill Pants Sets Due to Choking Hazard; Sold Exclusively at Marshalls
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Tommy Bahama brand brown stretch twill children's pants with rib cuffs and with a drawstring that were sold as part of a set with either a blue or green butter fleece crewneck top with an embroidered winking smiley face by the left shoulder. The pants were sold in sizes 18M to 5T. The sewn-in labe…
hazards: The plastic pieces on the end of the pants' faux drawstrings can come loose and fall off, posing a choking hazard.
products: Tommy Bahama brand children's pants sets
units: About 14,500 (In addition, about 720 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Foldable Bistro Set Chairs Due to Fall Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves wooden folding chairs sold as part of three-piece foldable bistro sets under the Panama Jack and House & Garden brands. The sets consist of one small wooden table and two foldable chairs made with wood frames and black, anthracite, navy blue, beige, olive green, or off-white roping on the seat and…
hazards: The wooden frame of the chair can break or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
injuries: The firm has received seven reports of the wooden frame of the chair breaking or collapsing when a consumer was seated on the chair, including four reports of minor injuries.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Egg Chairs Due to Fall Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, T.J. Maxx, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves nest swing egg chairs sold under the Tommy Bahama and Martha Stewart brands. The chairs have a metal circle base that attaches to a metal pole, from which the oval-shaped wicker chairs hang by hook and chain. The chairs were sold with a round cushion for seating and a hangtag with the brand name "…
hazards: The chairs can tip over or collapse when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
injuries: The firm has received 27 reports of the chairs collapsing or tipping over, including 19 reports of injuries, including cuts, scrapes, soreness and one report of broken ribs and a collapsed lung.

sampleRecalls.title: TJX Recalls Menorahs Due to Fire Hazard; Sold at Marshalls, HomeGoods and Homesense Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves resin and medium-density fiberboard ("MDF") menorahs with inserts for nine candles. The menorahs were sold in the following shapes: llama, brontosaurus, stegosaurus, T-rex, castle, rainbow, rainbow platform and emojis. The menorahs were sold with a white hangtag with an image of a blue and silver …
hazards: The resin and medium-density fiberboard ("MDF") menorahs can burn or catch fire when holding lit candles, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: TJX has received three reports in the U.S. of the menorahs burning or catching on fire when holding lit candles. No injuries or property damage have been reported.
